channel.title: Drupal AI
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal AI.

item.title: #3601376: Allow .drupalaibp.json to append or replace the welcome text
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601376 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

Projects shipping a .drupalaibp.json config can now customize the "you're all set" summary that the one-line installer prints at the end of a successful build. Two optional keys are available: append_welcome_text adds extra paragraphs after the standard summary (site URL, admin login, handy commands), and replace_welcome_text replaces the entire summary with custom content. Both keys work for Drupal and non-Drupal builds. When both are set, replace_welcome_text wins and append_welcome_text is ignored. The feature is documented in docs/drupalaibp-json.md including a full token and color reference.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: Two new .drupalaibp.json keys: append_welcome_text and replace_welcome_text, with {TOKEN} and color-code substitution in both.

Technical details

Both keys are read by the new _config_read_welcome_text function in src/lib/config/load_config.sh into APPEND_WELCOME_TEXT and REPLACE_WELCOME_TEXT bash arrays. Each key accepts either a bare string or an array of strings (paragraphs); the reader handles both shapes.

print_welcome in src/lib/steps/print_welcome.sh checks REPLACE_WELCOME_TEXT first: when the array is non-empty it iterates and prints each paragraph through _welcome_text_substitute, then returns before any of the standard summary is built. If only APPEND_WELCOME_TEXT is set, the standard summary prints in full and the extra paragraphs are appended at the end via the same helper.

The new helper src/lib/steps/_welcome_text_substitute.sh performs {TOKEN} substitution. Built-in tokens are {PROJECT_NAME}, {SITE_URL}, {DRUPAL_USERNAME}, {DRUPAL_PASSWORD}, and {DRUPAL_LOGIN_URL}. Any key from the config's questions array is also available by its env name. Color tokens ({BOLD}, {DIM}, {GREEN}, {YELLOW}, {RED}, {RESET}) expand to ANSI codes only when stdout is a real TTY (gated by _welcome_text_colors_enabled which wraps [[ -t 1 ]]); they collapse to nothing in piped or redirected output. An unrecognized token is left untouched rather than silently deleted, surfacing typos in the terminal output. Every paragraph receives an automatic trailing RESET when colors are enabled so an unclosed color token cannot bleed into subsequent output. Both new keys are registered in DRUPALAIBP_KNOWN_KEYS so the unknown-key warning does not fire on valid configs.

Upgrade

No changes required for existing configs; both keys are optional and the default behavior (standard summary, no appended or replaced text) is unchanged.

To append project-specific instructions after the standard summary:

{
  "append_welcome_text": [
    "Project docs: https://example.com/handbook",
    "Ask in #project-support if you get stuck."
  ]
}

To replace the entire summary with custom content (accepts a bare string or array):

{
  "replace_welcome_text": "{BOLD}{GREEN}Welcome to the ACME demo!{RESET} Run `ddev launch` and log in with admin / admin."
}

Tokens from questions[].env answers are available in both keys, e.g. {SITE_FLAVOR} when a question with "env": "SITE_FLAVOR" is defined. Unknown tokens are left verbatim in the output rather than silently removed.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 16, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601363: Add --config-path to install from a local, HTTPS, or repo-hosted .drupalaibp.json
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601363 · 1 contributor · 4 comments

Summary

The setup-site command gains a --config-path <dir> flag, a third way to point the installer at a .drupalaibp.json alongside the existing --global-config (a named file in ~/.drupalaibp/configs/) and --config-url (a git-cloned repo). Passing a local directory path — such as a folder checked into the current project — loads its root .drupalaibp.json, drives the build from it, and resolves sibling hook scripts and copy_paths assets relative to that directory. This lets teams share a config by committing it to their repo and passing its directory at build time, without copying the file into each developer's global config store. The flag is local-only and intentionally has no network or URL support; for remote configs, --config-url is the appropriate tool.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New --config-path <dir> flag on setup-site builds from a .drupalaibp.json in a local directory, mutually exclusive with --config-url and --global-config.

Technical details

The implementation lives in two new files: src/lib/config/_resolve_config_path_source.sh (_resolve_config_path_source) validates that the argument is an existing directory containing a .drupalaibp.json, then sets RESOLVED_CONFIG_SOURCE to its absolute path. src/lib/config/load_config_path.sh (load_config_path) calls that resolver and then hands the resolved path to the existing drupalaibp_load machinery, which populates all choice globals and registers config hooks exactly as it does for other load paths. setup_site_command.sh dispatches to load_config_path when --config-path is present and PROJECT_CONTEXT == none.

Compared to --global-config, --config-path sets only USE_CONFIG=1, not YOLO=1: the config's own questions still prompt interactively unless --yolo is passed separately. CONFIG_BASEDIR is set to the given directory (via the normal drupalaibp_load call), so hooks[].script and copy_paths entries resolve relative to it and are copied into the project by the existing _copy_hook_scripts/_copy_config_paths machinery. No key restriction, trust gate, or HTTPS fetch is involved; those concerns belong to --config-url.

--config-path is mutually exclusive with --config-url and --global-config; passing more than one of the three causes an immediate die. The flag is silently rejected in management mode (it only applies when PROJECT_CONTEXT == none). The bats suite covers the happy path, CONFIG_BASEDIR positioning, a missing .drupalaibp.json, and a nonexistent directory in tests/bats/lib/config/load_config_path.bats and _resolve_config_path_source.bats.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 9, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601371: Make the build script only move the main script on tags
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601371 · 1 contributor · 3 comments

Summary

The one-line installer's GitLab Pages publish step now fires only when a Git tag is pushed. Before this change the CI pages job ran on every merge to the default branch, meaning the public URL https://project.pages.drupalcode.org/one_line_installer/drupalaibp could serve unreleased, in-progress work to anyone running the one-liner. After this change that URL always reflects the most recent tagged release. The workflow for maintainers is now: merge the change, then tag the commit to publish it.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The pages job in .gitlab-ci.yml gained a rules: - if: '$CI_COMMIT_TAG' guard. The job builds the MkDocs docs site and copies drupalaibp alongside it (cp drupalaibp public/drupalaibp), then publishes the whole public/ directory to GitLab Pages.

The entire job is tag-gated rather than just the cp line because a Pages deploy replaces the whole site: a docs-only deploy from the default branch would publish a public/ directory without drupalaibp in it, deleting the installer from the live URL until the next tag. Keeping docs and script together and shipping both only on a tag avoids that window.

The bashly_verify job still runs on every pipeline (merge or tag), so drift between src/ and the committed drupalaibp is caught continuously. The tag pipeline runs bashly_verify as a prerequisite for pages (via needs), so a drifted script cannot be published even if a tag is pushed without regenerating.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 14, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601370: Agent Ready Drupal Build Kit
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601370 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The one_line_installer now offers the Agent-Ready Drupal Build Kit as an optional extra during setup. When selected, the installer adds a pre-packaged agent skill to every coding agent the user chose (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode), giving the agent a defined workflow for content modelling, verification, and review-packet generation when rebuilding an existing website as a Drupal CMS site. The extra is opt-in (not installed by default) and requires the Drupal CMS profile; choosing it on a standard or minimal profile skips installation and prints a prominent warning.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New agent_ready_drupal_build_kit installer extra: selectable via --extras agent_ready_drupal_build_kit or the interactive menu; requires the Drupal CMS profile (cms or template)

Technical details

The extra is delivered as src/lib/extras/extra_agent_ready_drupal_build_kit.sh. The installer discovers extras at runtime by scanning the function namespace for extra_<id>_meta symbols (see _extras_discover.sh), so no prompt, global, or pipeline change was needed beyond dropping in the new file.

Unlike the other extras (dtk, droost, tool_api), this one installs no Drupal module. It calls npx --yes skills add 'https://github.com/scottfalconer/agent-ready-drupal-build-kit' inside the DDEV web container, iterating over _skill_agent_targets to register the skill for each chosen agent. After installation it appends a note to the managed block in AGENTS.md.

The extra_agent_ready_drupal_build_kit_meta() function omits the default flag so the extra starts unticked in the interactive menu and is not installed under --yolo / unattended mode. A profile check gates execution: PROFILE must be cms or template (both scaffold drupal/cms); any other profile causes _build_kit_skipped_banner to print a bold-red ASCII block and returns early. The extra can also be selected non-interactively with --extras agent_ready_drupal_build_kit or added on top of the defaults with --add-extras agent_ready_drupal_build_kit.

Upgrade

No upgrade steps needed for existing sites. To use the new extra on a fresh install:

# Interactive menu — the extra appears as an opt-in choice
bash <(curl -fsSL https://project.pages.drupalcode.org/one_line_installer/drupalaibp)

# Or select it non-interactively (Drupal CMS profile is required)
drupalaibp --profile cms --extras agent_ready_drupal_build_kit

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 14, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601332: Adopt Bashly (bashly.dev) to generate the installer from a single YAML-defined CLI
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major task
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601332 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The one_line_installer project adopted Bashly as its build framework, replacing roughly 750 lines of hand-written bash spread across four files with a single generated script called drupalaibp. The committed drupalaibp file (around 4,900 lines) is generated from a src/bashly.yml CLI definition plus per-command and shared-library partials in src/lib/. End users run the same bash <(curl …) invocation; they now target the drupalaibp script rather than the old install.sh. Maintainers who regenerate the script need the bashly Ruby gem (version pinned in .bashly-version). A CI job (bashly_verify) fails if the committed script ever drifts from src/.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New .drupalaibp.json config schema for unattended, config-driven builds with provisioning, custom questions, and hook scripts at named pipeline events.

Technical details

The old installer duplicated step/ok/warn/die helpers and ANSI color constants across install.sh, install-macos.sh, install-linux.sh, and github-push.sh. Bashly solves this by injecting a shared library into the generated bundle. Each shared helper now lives once: src/lib/shared/colors.sh, src/lib/shared/output_helpers.sh, src/lib/shared/select_one.sh, src/lib/shared/select_many.sh, and so on.

The CLI is structured as three subcommands defined in src/bashly.yml: setup-site (the default, covering fresh installs and existing-project detection), install (registers the tool as a global drupal-setup command in ~/.local/bin), and delete (removes a DDEV project). Automatic --help, --version, and flag validation come from Bashly's generated argument parser, which requires bash 4. A src/lib/core/00_require_bash.sh shim re-execs under a newer bash when macOS ships bash 3.2.

The Bashly adoption also carried a significant feature expansion. A .drupalaibp.json config format was added (src/lib/config/load_config.sh, src/lib/config/hooks.sh) that lets maintainers drive unattended builds from a JSON file declaring profile, coding agents, skill repos, Composer packages, modules, recipes, DDEV add-ons, custom interactive questions, and hook scripts fired at named pipeline events (ddev_started, coding_agent_added, etc.). The config-export path (drupalaibp_export) serializes the interactive choices back to JSON in pure bash without a jq runtime dependency. The --config-url flag clones an external git repo containing a .drupalaibp.json and uses it as the build config.

Upgrade

Users previously invoking the old install.sh entry point should switch to drupalaibp:

# Old (1.0.x)
bash <(curl -fsSL https://git.drupalcode.org/project/one_line_installer/-/raw/1.0.x/install.sh)

# New (2.0.x)
bash <(curl -fsSL https://project.pages.drupalcode.org/one_line_installer/drupalaibp)

Maintainers who need to regenerate the committed script require the Bashly gem at the pinned version:

gem install bashly -v "$(cat .bashly-version)"
bin/generate   # regenerates drupalaibp from src/

The -yolo flag from 1.0.x is now --yolo (double-dash). Config-driven builds use a .drupalaibp.json placed in the launch directory; see docs/config.md and docs/examples/ for the full schema.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 1, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • unqunq (DigitalTrotter)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601367: Fix the viewport issue
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601367 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The one_line_installer's interactive menus (select_one for single-choice and select_many for checkbox selections) would corrupt the terminal display whenever the option list was taller than the terminal window. Each keypress would shove the entire display further up the screen instead of moving just the cursor. This is now fixed: menus clamp themselves to a scrollable viewport sized to the terminal height, showing only a window of options at a time with "↑ N more" / "↓ N more" indicators when the list is clipped. A related wrapping bug is also fixed: labels longer than the terminal width would wrap onto a second physical line, throwing off the repaint's cursor-up jump; labels are now truncated with an ellipsis and terminal line-wrap is disabled for the menu's duration.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Both menus repaint by issuing \033[<block>A to jump the cursor back up over the block they last drew, counting option rows. When the full option list exceeds the terminal height, printing the last row scrolls the buffer: the list no longer starts <block> rows above the cursor, so the jump lands on the wrong row and each subsequent repaint pushes the window further up. The fix is in src/lib/core/_menu_viewport.sh, which introduces five helpers used by both selectors.

_menu_term_height and _menu_term_width read terminal dimensions via stty size 2>/dev/null </dev/tty, falling back to 24 and 80 when there is no terminal. _menu_viewport <n> computes three globals: MENU_VIEW (option rows to paint), MENU_CLIPPED (1 when the list is clipped), and MENU_OFFSET (index of the first visible option). Available rows are terminal_height - MENU_CHROME_LINES where MENU_CHROME_LINES=3 accounts for the prompt, the key-hint line, and one spare row so the block never ends on the very last screen line. When clipped, MENU_VIEW is reduced by 2 to leave room for the indicator lines, which are part of the counted block. _menu_scroll <cur> <n> is called before every repaint and slides MENU_OFFSET so the highlighted row stays inside [MENU_OFFSET, MENU_OFFSET + MENU_VIEW). _menu_more_line draws the "↑/↓ N more" line, printing a blank-but-cleared line when nothing is hidden in that direction, so the block height remains constant and the cursor-up jump stays exact.

The second bug — a label wider than the terminal wrapping onto a second physical row — is addressed by _menu_fit <text> <reserve>, which truncates the label to _menu_term_width minus the prefix width and appends …. Both select_one and select_many additionally disable terminal line-wrap with \033[?7l for the duration of the menu and restore it with \033[?7h on exit, as a second safeguard. The skills menu was the concrete trigger: its option labels ran up to 111 columns on an 80-column terminal.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 14, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601369: Add the possibility to add one skill or extra to the default
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601369 · 1 contributor · 2 comments

Summary

The one_line_installer tool now supports --add-skills and --add-extras flags that extend the default agent skills and extra Drupal installations rather than replacing them. Previously, using --skills or --extras required listing every value you wanted, which would discard the defaults. The new additive flags let you say "give me the defaults plus these specific additions" without having to repeat the full default list. Both flags also compose with a loaded config: --add-skills extends a config's skills list, and --add-extras extends a config's extras rather than overwriting them.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New --add-skills and --add-extras CLI flags added to setup-site; they extend defaults (or a loaded config) rather than replacing them.

Technical details

Two new flags are declared in src/bashly.yml and processed in apply_cli_flags() inside src/lib/shared/prompts/apply_cli_flags.sh.

--add-skills accepts a comma-separated list of 'repo|skill' specifiers. When applied, it reads the default skill specs via _default_skill_specs() (or the config's SKILLS_SPEC array when USE_CONFIG=1), normalizes each added entry through _skill_spec_normalize(), then merges them with the base list using the new _merge_added() helper. The result is stored back in SKILLS_SPEC and SKILLS_SET=1 is set, which causes choose_skills to skip the interactive prompt.

--add-extras accepts a comma-separated list of extra IDs. It selects the base from whichever is already set: if --extras was also passed (or a config is active), the existing EXTRAS array is the base; otherwise _extras_default_ids() supplies the defaults. _merge_added() appends the new entries, deduplicating against the base in insertion order, and stores the result in EXTRAS with EXTRAS_SET=1.

_merge_added() is a small helper that takes a CSV string plus a list of base items, walks both in order, and prints each unique item exactly once. It handles skill specs that contain | and * without word-splitting or glob expansion by never passing them through bare variable expansion.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 14, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3601361: Replace the experimental tool prompts with a generic, script-driven "Extra Drupal Installations" multi-select
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major unknown (0)
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3601361 · 1 contributor · 5 comments

Summary

The one-line installer now offers all optional Drupal tools through a single "Any extra Drupal installations?" multi-select prompt instead of separate yes/no questions for each tool. Three extras ship today: Drush Token Killer (dtk, pre-ticked and installed by default under --yolo), Tool API + Toolbelt (tool_api, opt-in), and the new Droost AI-agent MCP integration (droost, opt-in). Anyone using the old --tool-api flag must switch to --extras tool_api. Config files should use the new extra_scripts array key instead of per-tool booleans. Adding a new optional tool no longer requires editing prompts, globals, the pipeline, or config readers: dropping one file into src/lib/extras/ and regenerating is all that is needed.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: .drupalaibp.json gains extra_scripts (array of ids: dtk, tool_api, droost); the old per-tool boolean keys are no longer read.
  • API change: The --tool-api boolean flag is removed; use --extras tool_api (or a comma-separated list) instead.
  • New API: New extra_installed <id> event emitted after each successful extra install; new src/lib/extras/ convention for adding optional tools as extra_<id>_meta + extra_<id> function pairs.

Technical details

The old architecture had a dedicated prompt function (choose_toolbelt, choose_dtk, choose_tool_api), a per-tool global (TOOL_API, TOOLBELT, DTK), a per-tool install step (step_install_tool_api), and a per-tool boolean CLI flag (--tool-api) for every optional install. Each addition required changes across at least five places.

The new architecture introduces src/lib/extras/ as a convention. Each extra defines exactly two bash functions: extra_<id>_meta (prints Name|Description, or Name|Description|default to mark it as a default) and extra_<id> (performs the install using step/ok/warn and ddev/drush). The single _extras_discover function finds them at runtime via compgen -A function scanning the bash function namespace, because the entire script is bundled by Bashly and extras cannot be globbed off disk. choose_extras (registered on ask_questions at weight 45) builds one select_many menu from the discovered extras, pre-ticking defaults via MENU_PRESELECT, and sets the EXTRAS array of ids. The generic step_install_extras (pipeline slot 10, replacing step_install_tool_api) iterates EXTRAS, calls extra_<id>, and emits extra_installed <id> on success. Failures warn and continue.

The --tool-api boolean flag is replaced by --extras LIST (comma-separated ids). Presence of the flag, not its value, overrides the defaults: --extras "" is a valid explicit "install nothing." The .drupalaibp.json config schema gains extra_scripts (array of ids), read into EXTRAS by load_config.sh. Old per-tool boolean keys (toolbelt, dtk) are not read; the project documents this as part of its 2.0 schema clean break. export_config.sh writes extra_scripts with the selected ids rather than expanding packages into composer_projects/install_modules, so configs no longer need to track package names separately from the extras themselves. A new extra_installed event joins the event registry alongside ddev_addon_added and skill_added.

Also in this commit: the Bashly generator version is pinned to a .bashly-version file and a bin/generate wrapper enforces the pin, preventing CI failures caused by version drift between local and CI generators.

Upgrade

If you use the CLI flag, replace --tool-api with --extras tool_api:

# Before
drupalaibp --tool-api

# After
drupalaibp --extras tool_api
# Multiple extras (comma-separated)
drupalaibp --extras tool_api,droost
# Explicit "none" (overrides the dtk default)
drupalaibp --extras ""

If you have a .drupalaibp.json config that listed Tool API packages manually, replace those entries with the extra_scripts key:

// Before (manual package listing)
{
  "composer_projects": ["drupal/tool", "drupal/tool_belt"],
  "install_modules": ["tool", "tool_belt", "tool_belt_content", "..."],
  "composer_allow_plugins": ["tbachert/spi"]
}

// After
{
  "extra_scripts": ["tool_api"]
}

To add a new optional tool, create one file in src/lib/extras/ following this pattern, then run bin/generate:

# src/lib/extras/extra_mymodule.sh
extra_mymodule_meta() {
  printf '%s|%s\n' "My Module" "short description shown in the menu"
  # Add |default as a third field to pre-tick it
}

extra_mymodule() {
  ddev exec composer require drupal/mymodule --no-interaction || { warn "Could not require mymodule."; return 1; }
  ddev drush en -y mymodule || warn "Could not enable mymodule."
  ok "My Module installed."
}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 9, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3309122: Add a new dedicated permission for JSON API extra configurations
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 8.x-3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 7 files, +99/−11 · php +94/−10 · yml +5/−1
  • Diff: 5260dba
  • Discussion: #3309122 · 6 contributors · 16 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

JSON API Extras previously required the broad administer site configuration permission to access its configuration forms and manage resource config entities. This made it impossible to delegate JSON:API Extras configuration to a role without granting that role unrestricted access to every configuration on the site. A new, dedicated permission called administer json api extra configuration now controls access to these forms. An automatic database update (jsonapi_extras_update_9002) grants the new permission to every role that already held administer site configuration, so existing setups will continue working after running updates.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Permission change: New administer json api extra configuration permission replaces administer site configuration for all JSON API Extras configuration forms and resource config entities; run update.php or drush updb to migrate existing roles automatically.

Technical details

The change touches three areas in the codebase. First, jsonapi_extras.permissions.yml declares the new administer json api extra configuration permission with restrict access: true. Second, the route requirement in the routing YAML and the admin_permission annotation on the JsonapiResourceConfig config entity both switch from administer site configuration to the new permission, which gates both the settings form (/admin/config/services/jsonapi/extras) and all CRUD operations on the resource config entities. Third, jsonapi_extras_update_9002() in jsonapi_extras.install iterates over all user roles and calls grantPermission('administer json api extra configuration') on any role that already has administer site configuration, ensuring backward compatibility without manual intervention. A new kernel test JsonapiExtrasUpdateTest verifies that the update hook grants the permission only to qualifying roles.

Upgrade

Run drush updb (or visit /update.php) after updating the module. The update hook jsonapi_extras_update_9002 automatically grants the new permission to all roles that had administer site configuration.

If you have roles that should be able to manage JSON:API Extras configuration but do not have administer site configuration, grant the new permission to those roles manually:

// Via Drush or a one-time script:
$role = \Drupal\user\Entity\Role::load('your_role_id');
$role->grantPermission('administer json api extra configuration')->save();

Or grant it through the admin UI at /admin/people/permissions.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 12, 2022
  • Committed: July 13, 2026 (3 years later)

Key contributors

  • mingsong
  • bbrala (SWIS)
  • ankitv18 (Acquia)
  • jackwrfuller
  • ali-h

Collaboration

The issue was originally filed and patched by mingsong in September 2022. It sat idle for nearly two years until bbrala noted in May 2024 that the code had become outdated. ankitv18 then rebased the merge request and confirmed the pipelines were passing. After another year, bbrala added a kernel test covering the upgrade path before committing and closing the issue.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3582452: Add guardrail set selection to ai_search_block
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 7 files, +668/−3 · php +507 · yml +160/−2 · json +1/−1
  • Diff: a6c2d8f
  • Discussion: #3582452 · 3 contributors · 12 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The AI Search Block configuration form now includes a dedicated Guardrails section. Site builders can choose one of three modes: no guardrails, apply an existing saved ai_guardrail_set entity, or pick individual pre-generation and post-generation guardrails. The selected guardrail configuration is saved in block config, exported correctly, and is attached to every AI request that block makes, so compatible providers (such as LiteLLM) can enforce the configured safety policies. If guardrail entities are deleted or unavailable, stale IDs are filtered out at request time rather than causing errors. This requires the ai module at version 1.4 or later.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Requirements: drupal/ai minimum version raised from ^1.2 to ^1.4.
  • New API: New guardrail_mode, guardrail_set, pre_generate_guardrails, post_generate_guardrails, and guardrail_stop_threshold keys in block.settings.ai_search_block config schema.

Technical details

The SearchFormBlock plugin gains a guardrails fieldset with a guardrail_mode radio group (none, sets, individual). In sets mode, a select element lists all ai_guardrail_set entities; in individual mode, two checkboxes elements list all ai_guardrail entities for pre-generation and post-generation slots, along with a numeric guardrail_stop_threshold field. Empty-state markup (with links to create/manage entities) is shown when no entities exist, preventing confusing or broken UI states. calculateDependencies() is implemented on SearchFormBlock to record the selected ai_guardrail_set and ai_guardrail config entities as proper block config dependencies.

The actual guardrail attachment happens in the new private applyGuardrailSet() method on AiSearchBlockHelper, called just before ChatInput is passed to the provider. In sets mode it delegates to AiGuardrailHelper::applyGuardrailSetToChatInput(). In individual mode it loads and validates the selected guardrail IDs, then creates a transient unsaved AiGuardrailSet entity (identified by a hashed UUID prefix to avoid collisions), clones the ChatInput, and calls addGuardrailSet() on the clone. Non-existent guardrail IDs are intersected against actually-loaded entities before the transient set is built. The @ai.guardrail_helper service is injected into AiSearchBlockHelper via ai.services.yml. The drupal/ai requirement is bumped from ^1.2 to ^1.4 because addGuardrailSet() and getGuardrailSets() on ChatInput are only available from that version. A complete block.settings.ai_search_block config schema was also added, covering all existing and new settings. Kernel test coverage is provided in AiSearchBlockHelperGuardrailTest with five test cases covering sets mode, individual mode, empty selection, and stale-ID filtering.

Upgrade

Sites must update the drupal/ai dependency to ^1.4 before enabling this version of ai_search_block. In composer.json:

"require": {
    "drupal/ai": "^1.4"
}

No existing block configuration is affected: the new guardrail_mode key defaults to none, so existing blocks continue to send requests without guardrails unless explicitly reconfigured.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 31, 2026
  • Committed: July 10, 2026 (3 months later)

Collaboration

The issue was opened by nikro, who outlined the full design in the initial comment and indicated the work would target the 1.3.x planning cycle. abhisekmazumdar opened MR !44 and performed hands-on testing of all three guardrail modes, verifying config persistence and dependency recording. During review, abhisekmazumdar identified and pushed several fixes to the branch: the deprecated setGuardrailSet()/getGuardrailSet() calls were replaced with addGuardrailSet()/getGuardrailSets() in both AiSearchBlockHelper.php and the kernel test, two anonymous closures in uasort() calls were converted to arrow functions, a use statement was reordered alphabetically, and a @covers annotation was corrected to target the class rather than a private method. Nikro then pushed the final version-constraint correction (bumping ^1.3 to ^1.4 to match the API actually used) and merged to 1.1.x.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3583809: Add Text Classification support
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +52/−5 · php +51/−4 · json +1/−1
  • Diff: 2b16e6b
  • Discussion: #3583809 · 6 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

The ai_provider_huggingface module now supports the text_classification operation type introduced by the AI core module. Site builders can add a Hugging Face text-classification endpoint (such as distilbert/distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english) on the provider settings page and then use the Text Classification Explorer at /admin/config/ai/explorers/text_classification_generator. The module's drupal/ai dependency has been raised to ^1.4.x-dev@dev, which is the version that ships the text-classification operation type classes.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Requirements: Minimum drupal/ai version raised from ^1.3.x-dev@dev to ^1.4.x-dev@dev.
  • New API: New text_classification operation type available in HuggingfaceProvider, implementing TextClassificationInterface from drupal/ai 1.4.x.

Technical details

The HuggingfaceProvider class now implements TextClassificationInterface and lists text_classification in both its $supportedTypes map (with Hugging Face pipeline filter text-classification) and its getSupportedOperationTypes() return value. The new textClassification() method accepts a string or TextClassificationInput, calls HuggingfaceApi::textClassification(), decodes the JSON response, handles both single-item and array responses from the Inference API, and wraps each result in a TextClassificationItem before returning a TextClassificationOutput.

A pre-existing bug in HuggingfaceApi::textClassification() was fixed at the same time: the method always included 'parameters' => [] in the request payload. PHP's json_encode serializes an empty array as [] (a JSON list), which the Hugging Face Inference API rejects with a 400 error and the message 'list' object has no attribute 'setdefault'. The fix omits the parameters key entirely when the array is empty; when parameters are present they are cast to (object) so they serialize as a JSON object {}.

The composer.json dependency on drupal/ai was bumped from ^1.3.x-dev@dev to ^1.4.x-dev@dev because TextClassificationInterface, TextClassificationInput, TextClassificationItem, and TextClassificationOutput were added to AI core in 1.4.x via #3456768.

Upgrade

If your project depends on ai_provider_huggingface, update your composer.json to require at least drupal/ai ^1.4.x-dev@dev:

"require": {
    "drupal/ai": "^1.4.x-dev@dev"
}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 9, 2026
  • Committed: July 10, 2026 (3 months later)

Collaboration

During review, csakiistvan identified that the original MR had four failing CI jobs: the TextClassification* classes did not exist in the drupal/ai 1.3.x branch declared in composer.json, causing non-ignorable phpstan errors. csakiistvan filed a detailed testing report documenting the root cause and the two fixes needed (dependency bump and use statement reordering). After ahmad khader applied those fixes, jucs7 re-tested the full flow, confirmed CI was green and the empty-parameters 400 error was resolved, and moved the issue to RTBC.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3548526: Support File Handling
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +572/−2 · php +571/−1 · json +1/−1
  • Diff: 623d27c
  • Discussion: #3548526 · 6 contributors · 26 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

The OpenAI provider module for Drupal now supports the OpenAI Files API through the normalized AI module file-handling interface. Developers can upload files to OpenAI, attach them by remote ID to chat messages, download file content, and delete remote files. This enables use cases like document Q&A and fine-tuning data management without leaving the Drupal AI abstraction layer. Requires drupal/ai 1.4.2 or higher.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Requirements: Minimum drupal/ai version raised from ^1.4.0 to ^1.4.2
  • New API: OpenAiProvider now implements AiFileProviderInterface, adding uploadFile(), deleteFile(), downloadFile(), and supportsMimeType() methods for interacting with the OpenAI Files API.

Technical details

OpenAiProvider now implements AiFileProviderInterface (from drupal/ai 1.4.2+) and uses the FileApiTrait (Drupal\ai\Traits\OpenAi\FileApiTrait) to fulfil that interface. The trait provides uploadFile(), deleteFile(), downloadFile(), and supportsMimeType() methods backed by the OpenAI PHP SDK. MIME type eligibility is gated by purpose: batch and fine_tune accept text and JSON only, vision accepts image types, and user_data is unrestricted.

On the chat side, OpenAiProvider::chat() was extended to detect getRemoteFiles() on each ChatMessage and append {'type': 'file', 'file': {'file_id': $id}} content blocks to the outgoing API payload. This allows previously uploaded files to be referenced in a conversation turn without re-sending file bytes.

Error handling in uploadFile() maps "Request too large" API errors to AiRateLimitException and quota errors to AiQuotaException. The minimum drupal/ai constraint in composer.json was tightened from ^1.4.0 to ^1.4.2, which is the release that introduced AiFileProviderInterface and FileApiTrait in the base module (see #3546954).

Upgrade

No changes required for existing code. To use file handling, obtain the provider as AiFileProviderInterface and call its methods:

use Drupal\ai\AiFileProviderInterface;

// Upload a file (returns the AiFile entity with remote ID set).
/** @var AiFileProviderInterface $provider */
$aiFile = $this->aiFileManager->upload('public://docs/report.pdf', 1, 'openai');

// Reference the uploaded file in a chat message.
$message = new ChatMessage('user', 'Summarize this document.');
$message->addRemoteFile($aiFile->getRemoteId());
$input = new ChatInput([$message]);
$response = $provider->chat($input, 'gpt-4o')->getNormalized();

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 24, 2025
  • Committed: July 7, 2026 (9 months later)

Collaboration

b_sharpe demonstrated the feature working end-to-end in a comment showing file upload followed by referencing the remote ID in a chat message. marcus_johansson steered the implementation toward using the AiModelCapability enum for capability checks and suggested moving the file trait into the base AI module rather than keeping it in the provider, which is what ultimately shipped. b_sharpe subsequently rebased the MR against the 1.3.x branch after drupal/ai 1.4.2 landed with the normalized file API.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3600886: Add an "AI audio" field validation constraint (speech-to-text)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 10 files, +328/−8 · php +322/−7 · md +6/−1
  • Diff: 94e5c2d
  • Discussion: #3600886 · 2 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The ai_validations module gains a seventh field validation rule: AI audio constraint. Site builders can now attach this rule to a File field that accepts audio uploads. When an editor saves a node, the constraint first transcribes the uploaded audio through a configurable speech-to-text provider, then evaluates that transcript with a separate chat provider using the same XTRUE/XFALSE prompt protocol the existing text and image constraints already use. If the chat provider returns XFALSE, the node is rejected and the configured error message is shown.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New transcribeAudio() and buildProviderDefaultValue(string $configKey) helpers on AiConstraintValidatorBase and AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase; loadImageFile() renamed to loadFile() on AiConstraintValidatorBase.

Technical details

Three new files implement the feature: AiAudioConstraintFieldValidationRule (the @FieldValidationRule plugin with id ai_audio_constraint_rule), AiAudioConstraint (the Symfony @Constraint with id AiAudioPrompt), and AiAudioConstraintValidator (the validator). The validator resolves both provider options, calls transcribeAudio() to get a string, then passes it straight into runBooleanChat() to obtain a boolean verdict.

Two base-class changes support the new plugin and clean up the existing ones. In AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase, the private logic formerly in buildModelDefaultValue() was extracted into a new buildProviderDefaultValue(string $configKey) method. buildModelDefaultValue() now delegates to it, so subclasses that need a second provider selector (as AiAudioConstraintFieldValidationRule does for its chat_provider key) can call buildProviderDefaultValue() with any config key without duplicating the decode logic. In AiConstraintValidatorBase, loadImageFile() was renamed to loadFile() to reflect that it handles any file type, and a new transcribeAudio(mixed $data, string $option) helper was added alongside the existing loadFile() and runBooleanChat() helpers. All three existing image-constraint validators (AiImageConstraintValidator, AiImageClassificationConstraintValidator, AiObjectDetectionConstraintValidator) were updated to call loadFile() instead of loadImageFile().

Upgrade

Custom validators that extend AiConstraintValidatorBase and call loadImageFile() must rename the call to loadFile():

// Before
$file = $this->loadImageFile($data);

// After
$file = $this->loadFile($data);

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 15, 2026
  • Committed: July 1, 2026 (16 days later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • abhisekmazumdar (Dropsolid)

Collaboration

abhisekmazumdar documented two design choices beyond the original plan in comment #16647960: exposing two independent ai_provider_configuration selectors (speech-to-text and chat) rather than a single one, because the two operation types are commonly served by different providers, and adding transcribeAudio() to the shared base class rather than scoping it to the new validator alone, so future constraints can reuse it without duplication. marcus_johansson reviewed the patch, suggested one minor refinement, and marked it ready to commit.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3595515: Add AI Text Classification field validation rule (mirror of AI Image Classification)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +1/−0 · php +1
  • Diff: c0270a8
  • Discussion: #3595515 · 2 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The ai_validations module now ships a fifth field validation rule: AI text classification constraint. Site owners can attach it to any text or string field and configure an AI provider model, a classification tag to block (e.g. "spam"), a matching strategy (exact, contains, or case-insensitive contains), a minimum confidence threshold (0–1), a "model not available" behavior (skip or fail), and a custom error message. When a content form is submitted, the validator sends the field value to the chosen AI provider's text-classification operation and adds a violation if the returned label matches the configured tag at or above the confidence threshold. This mirrors the behavior of the existing AI image classification rule.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New AiTextClassificationConstraint, AiTextClassificationConstraintValidator, and AiTextClassificationConstraintFieldValidationRule plugin classes; resolveProviderOption() in AiConstraintValidatorBase gains an optional $violateOnMissing bool parameter (default TRUE, fully backward-compatible).

Technical details

Three new plugin files implement the feature. AiTextClassificationConstraintFieldValidationRule (plugin id ai_text_classification_constraint_rule) extends AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase and exposes the config form; it returns 'AiTextClassification' from getConstraintName(). AiTextClassificationConstraint is the Symfony @Constraint class (id AiTextClassification) holding properties message, model, tag, finder, minimum, and na. AiTextClassificationConstraintValidator extends AiConstraintValidatorBase; its validate() method wraps the field value in TextClassificationInput, calls $provider->textClassification($input, $model), and iterates the normalized result through the shared labelMatchesFinder() / confidence comparison logic inherited from the base class.

During development a bug was found: when no text_classification provider was configured and the rule was set to na: skip, the validator was still raising a violation because resolveProviderOption() in AiConstraintValidatorBase unconditionally called addViolation() on a missing provider. The fix adds a $violateOnMissing boolean parameter (default TRUE) to resolveProviderOption(); the text classification validator passes $constraint->na !== 'skip' so the violation is suppressed when graceful degradation is requested.

Test coverage is provided by a new AiTextClassificationConstraintValidatorTest Kernel test. Because AiProviderPluginManager is declared final and cannot be mocked directly, a helper testing module (ai_validations_test) was added, containing ClassifierTestProvider (extending EchoProvider) whose textClassification() output is driven by Drupal state. This lets the Kernel test exercise every branch: match above threshold, match below threshold, label mismatch, contains-finder, empty value short-circuit, missing model with na: fail, provider exception with na: fail, and provider exception with na: skip. A FunctionalJavascript test case was also added to assert the selected model persists correctly through the config form save cycle.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 10, 2026
  • Committed: July 1, 2026 (20 days and 5 commits later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • abhisekmazumdar (Dropsolid)

Collaboration

During MR review, abhisekmazumdar discovered a runtime bug: with na set to skip, the validator still raised a violation when no provider was found, because the shared resolveProviderOption() helper always called addViolation(). They described the root cause in a comment and fixed it by adding a $violateOnMissing parameter to resolveProviderOption() so callers can suppress the violation when graceful degradation is configured. They also documented why a Unit test was not viable (the final AiProviderPluginManager) and why they introduced the ai_validations_test helper module instead.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3600885: Add an "AI moderation" field validation constraint
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +25/−2 · php +25/−2
  • Diff: 6abd71b
  • Discussion: #3600885 · 2 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The ai_validations module gains a fifth field validation rule: "AI moderation constraint." Site builders can attach it to any text field and it will call the AI module's native moderation() operation on form save, blocking submission when the provider flags the content as harmful. The rule's configuration lets you choose a provider and model, optionally restrict failures to specific moderation categories (e.g. hate,violence,sexual), and set a confidence threshold for those categories. If the moderation provider throws an exception, the error is logged and the field fails validation rather than crashing the site.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New ai_moderation_constraint_rule field validation rule plugin that screens text fields using the AI module's moderation() operation type.

Technical details

Three new classes implement the constraint following the existing plugin pattern. AiModerationConstraintFieldValidationRule (plugin id ai_moderation_constraint_rule) provides the admin config form with an ai_provider_configuration element for provider/model selection, a free-text categories field (comma-separated, blank means any flagged category triggers a violation), and a threshold float field (0–1, default 0.5) applied when specific categories are configured. AiModerationConstraint is the constraint data class holding $categories, $provider, and $threshold. AiModerationConstraintValidator resolves the provider, skips empty/null values using $data === NULL || (string) $data === '' (fixing a prior empty() false-negative on 0), then calls $provider->moderation(new ModerationInput($data), $model)->getNormalized(). If isFlagged() is true and no category filter is set, a violation is added immediately. With categories configured, the validator reads scores from getInformation() and adds a violation only when a watched category's score is numeric and exceeds the configured threshold. Provider exceptions are caught, logged via the ai_validations logger channel, and treated as a violation.

A separate fix in AiConstraintValidatorBase::runBooleanChat() wraps the $provider->chat() call in a try/catch that returns FALSE on any exception. Previously, providers that reject inputs via their own safety filter (before returning a response) would throw an uncaught exception and produce a 500 page; all three existing chat-based validators (text prompt, image prompt, audio) are affected by this fix.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 15, 2026
  • Committed: July 1, 2026 (16 days and 5 commits later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • abhisekmazumdar (Dropsolid)

Collaboration

abhisekmazumdar contributed the implementation and noted a deliberate design choice in a comment: the original issue proposed a confidence threshold, but getInformation() returns provider-dependent structures (floats on OpenAI, different shapes elsewhere), so a cross-provider numeric threshold was initially skipped. After review rounds with marcus_johansson, the threshold was re-added with proper is_numeric() guarding so it only applies when the value is a comparable float. abhisekmazumdar also caught and fixed a separate fatal-error bug in runBooleanChat() while preparing a demo, which was rolled into the same branch.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3593019: Add ImageToImage possibilities
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +183/−1 · php +149/−1 · yml +34
  • Diff: e0a64ca
  • Discussion: #3593019 · 4 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The OpenAI AI provider module now supports the image_to_image operation type, enabling developers to use OpenAI's image editing endpoint (used by gpt-image-1 and dall-e-2) to modify existing images with a text prompt and an optional mask. Previously the module only supported generating images from text (text_to_image). Sites that want to offer image editing functionality through the AI module ecosystem can now wire up these models without building a custom provider.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New image_to_image operation type on OpenAiProvider, implementing ImageToImageInterface with support for prompt-driven image editing and optional mask via OpenAI's images-edit endpoint.

Technical details

OpenAiProvider now implements ImageToImageInterface and uses ImageToImageTrait. The new imageToImage() method only accepts an ImageToImageInput object (plain string or array inputs throw AiResponseErrorException), which carries the source image binary, an optional mask ImageFile, and the edit prompt. Because the OpenAI PHP SDK uploads files as multipart form data, the method writes the image (and mask, if supplied) to temporary:// paths via fileSystem->saveData(), opens them as PHP file resources, and passes them to $this->client->images()->edit(). A finally block guarantees the temporary files and open handles are cleaned up regardless of outcome. The response bodies are decoded from base64 JSON; output MIME type defaults to image/png but switches to image/jpeg or image/webp when output_format is set in the configuration payload. The provider declares requiresImageToImagePrompt() as TRUE (the edits endpoint always requires a prompt) and hasImageToImageMask() as TRUE. Model filtering in the provider's model-listing logic restricts image_to_image to models matching dall-e-2 or gpt-image-*, explicitly excluding dall-e-3 which cannot edit images. The default model recommendation for this operation type is gpt-image-1. api_defaults.yml gained an image_to_image section covering n (1–10) and size configuration keys.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 1, 2026
  • Committed: June 26, 2026 (24 days later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • merilainen (Mearra (formerly known as Wunder))

Collaboration

During review, merilainen approved the change and made a small follow-up code adjustment, removing the response_format key from the image_to_image section of api_defaults.yml after marcus_johansson flagged it as incorrect. merilainen then marked the issue RTBC and marcus_johansson merged it.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590873: Dall-E 3 has been deprecated and should be removed as default
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.2.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +29/−4 · php +24/−1 · yml +5/−3
  • Discussion: #3590873 · 4 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

OpenAI deprecated the Dall-E 3 image model on 12 May 2026. This change updates the AI Provider OpenAI module to replace dall-e-3 with gpt-image-1 as the default text_to_image model, and revises the available image sizes to match those supported by gpt-image-1. Existing sites that had dall-e-3 set automatically as their default will be migrated to gpt-image-1 when they run database updates. Sites where an administrator explicitly chose dall-e-3 are not affected by the update hook.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Database update: ai_provider_openai_update_10002 migrates any stored dall-e-3 default to gpt-image-1 in ai.settings.
  • Config change: Default text_to_image model in ai.settings changes from dall-e-3 to gpt-image-1; available size options updated to match gpt-image-1 (removes 256x256 and 512x512, adds portrait and landscape variants).

Technical details

In OpenAiProvider, the hardcoded default for the text_to_image operation changes from 'dall-e-3' to 'gpt-image-1'. The accompanying configuration schema removes the Dall-E 3 size options (256x256, 512x512) and replaces them with the sizes gpt-image-1 supports: 1024x1024, 1024x1536, 1536x1024, 1024x1792, and 1792x1024, with a new default of 1024x1024.

A new ai_provider_openai_update_10002() hook handles existing installations. It reads ai.settings and checks whether default_providers['text_to_image']['model_id'] is exactly dall-e-3. If so, it rewrites that key to gpt-image-1 and saves the config. Sites that manually selected dall-e-3 retain their stored value; the hook only replaces the old auto-assigned default.

Upgrade

Run database updates after installing the new module version:

drush updb

If your site had dall-e-3 set as the automatic default, it will be migrated to gpt-image-1. If you explicitly configured dall-e-3 and wish to continue using it while OpenAI still supports the model, no automatic change will be made, but you should plan to select a supported model before OpenAI fully removes dall-e-3.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 19, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • abhisekmazumdar (Dropsolid)
  • danrod (OPTASY)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3604032: Add static_list completion providers to example prompt argument configs and cover them with tests
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3604032 · 1 contributor · 3 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The mcp_server_examples module now ships example prompt argument configurations that expose static_list completion providers, enabling MCP clients to autocomplete valid values for arguments such as skill_level, court_surface, focus_area, and others. Previously these arguments described their valid values only in prose; they now carry machine-readable completion data that MCP clients can query. Functional test coverage was added at the same time to verify each configured argument through the completion/complete endpoint.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Each affected prompt config YAML received one or more completion_providers blocks using plugin_id: static_list, listing the exact accepted values. The affected arguments span six prompt configs: comprehensive_lesson.lesson_focus, match_strategy.opponent_type and court_surface, multi_provider_prompt.opponent_type (which was also missing three of its four values), tennis_resources.topic, tennis_tip_basic.skill_level and focus_area, and test_with_args.skill_level and surface. No schema changes were needed because mcp_server.mcp_prompt_config.* already declared completion_providers as a supported key.

The new assertPromptArgumentCompletions() method in McpServerExamplesFunctionalTest iterates over nine argument cases, calls the completion/complete MCP endpoint with an empty argument value, and asserts that completion.values, completion.total, and completion.hasMore all match the configured static list. The method is called from the existing testMcpServerExamples() entry point, so it runs as part of the standard functional test suite.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 18, 2026

Key contributors

  • e0ipso (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586397: Switch FieldValidationRule plugins to the Provider Configuration Form Element
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 14 files, +856/−449 · php +851/−449 · neon +4 · txt +1
  • Diff: 66d9d6f
  • Discussion: #3586397 · 4 contributors · 23 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The AI Validations module previously used a plain dropdown for selecting a specific AI provider and model in each validation rule, with no way to fall back to the site's configured default provider. This made it impossible to create portable recipes or shared configurations without hardcoding a provider. Each of the four AI FieldValidationRule plugins now uses the AI module's reusable ai_provider_configuration form element, which surfaces a "Default" option that automatically falls back to whichever provider the site administrator has configured as the default for the relevant operation type. Existing saved rule configurations using a hardcoded provider__model string continue to work without any data migration.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: Two new public abstract base classes: AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase for rule plugins and AiConstraintValidatorBase for constraint validators, intended for custom AI validation rule implementations.

Technical details

Two new abstract base classes consolidate the shared code that was previously duplicated across each plugin. AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase (in src/AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase.php) extends ConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase and centralizes ai.provider service injection, plus the helpers buildModelDefaultValue() (decodes a stored provider__model string into the structured array the ai_provider_configuration element expects as #default_value) and extractStoredModel() (normalizes the submitted element value back into a provider__model string or empty string). Subclasses implement the abstract getOperationType() method and may override getModelConfigKey() to preserve legacy config key names: AiTextConstraintFieldValidationRule and AiImageConstraintFieldValidationRule store under provider, while AiImageClassificationConstraintFieldValidationRule and AiObjectDetectionConstraintFieldValidationRule use model.

AiConstraintValidatorBase (in src/AiConstraintValidatorBase.php) extends ConstraintValidator and provides three shared helpers used by all four validators: resolveProviderOption() resolves the stored provider__model option or falls back to getDefaultProviderForOperationType() on the ai.provider plugin manager; loadImageFile() loads a file entity by ID with consistent violation handling; runBooleanChat() encapsulates the XTRUE/XFALSE prompt protocol; and labelMatchesFinder() applies the exact/contains/substring matching strategies. A previously missed gap was fixed during review: AiObjectDetectionConstraintValidator was still reading $constraint->model directly instead of calling resolveProviderOption(), meaning it could not fall back to the configured default. It now matches the other three validators.

A new hook_form_FORM_ID_alter() in ai_validations.module (with submit handler _ai_validations_sync_rule_to_collection()) works around a latent field_validation bug exposed when the AJAX-driven ai_provider_configuration element is present: serialization during the AJAX round-trip causes the form controller's plugin instance to diverge from the rule set's plugin collection, silently dropping edits on final save. The workaround uses ReflectionProperty to push the form's modified instance back into the collection before save(). A @todo links to the upstream issue #3587461 for removal once the bug is fixed there.

Upgrade

Developers who have subclassed any of the four rule plugins directly should update their extends clause to use the new base class (or ConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase if the AI-specific helpers are not needed). The stored configuration format (provider__model string, or empty string for "Default") is unchanged, so no data migration is required.

// Before (example for a custom plugin extending one of the AI rule plugins):
class MyCustomAiRule extends AiTextConstraintFieldValidationRule { ... }

// After (extend the new shared base directly if you need provider config support):
class MyCustomAiRule extends AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase {
  protected function getOperationType(): string {
    return 'chat'; // return the relevant operation type
  }
}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 23, 2026
  • Committed: June 12, 2026 (1 month later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • ahmad-khalil-imagex
  • a.dmitriiev (1xINTERNET)
  • abhisekmazumdar (Dropsolid)

Collaboration

During review, abhisekmazumdar noticed that AiObjectDetectionConstraintValidator had been left out of the resolveProviderOption() migration: it was still reading $constraint->model directly, meaning object detection rules could not fall back to the site's default provider even after the other three validators were updated. abhisekmazumdar added the fix alongside a clarifying doc comment explaining why $strictConfigSchema = FALSE was set in the test class. ahmad-khalil-imagex also added substantial functional JavaScript test coverage after a.dmitriiev requested it, including a detailed account in comment #12 of an AJAX ordering gotcha: the provider select fires a rebuild that wipes sibling field values, so the helper primeRuleFormFields() must populate required fields before the provider is selected.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3595519: Depend on core recipe for image media type
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +1/−2 · yml +1/−1 · json +0/−1
  • Diff: b9938de
  • Discussion: #3595519 · 2 contributors · 9 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The ai_recipe_validations_image_safety recipe previously required the drupal_cms_media recipe as a dependency, which pulled in many unrelated packages and had a broken Composer version constraint (^1.0 while the package is now at 2.x). This made the recipe uninstallable via Composer. The dependency is now replaced with the leaner core/recipes/image_media_type recipe, which is what the recipe actually needs. The change also broadens compatibility so the recipe works on any Drupal site that uses the core image media type, not just Drupal CMS environments.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

In composer.json, the drupal/drupal_cms_media: ^1.0 require entry is removed entirely. In the recipe YAML, the recipes list entry drupal_cms_media is replaced with core/recipes/image_media_type. The drupal/core and other direct dependencies (drupal/ai, drupal/field_validation) are unchanged. The core/recipes/image_media_type recipe sets up the Media: Image bundle that this recipe's field validations target, so it satisfies the actual runtime requirement without the overhead of the full Drupal CMS media stack. A secondary effect noted in the thread is that the old ^1.0 constraint had no matching release (drupal_cms_media skipped 1.x entirely and went straight to 2.x), causing a hard Composer resolution failure before this fix.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 10, 2026
  • Committed: June 11, 2026 (1 day later)

Collaboration

a.dmitriiev noted in a follow-up comment that the change also resolved a concrete Composer installation failure: drupal/drupal_cms_media ^1.0 had no satisfying release because the package was only available at 2.x, making the recipe impossible to composer require at all on any current Drupal project.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
